content.claim: Since 2013, centralization of school maintenance under the Public Education Act and Klebelsberg Institution Maintenance Centre (KLIK) has curtailed municipal and institutional autonomy over hiring, budgets, and network planning decisions, making adaptation to local capacity constraints (teacher shortages, transport feasibility) difficult.
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Governance centralization is documented institutional fact, but its causal effect on specific track-level network resilience outcomes is inferred rather than measured
Local-level impact heterogeneity (urban vs. rural adaptation capacity) is not quantified
No direct comparison of network flexibility under pre-2013 decentralized structure
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
